title: Return JSON Error Responses and Implement API Rate Limiting
description:
## Context

When malformed requests are sent to the DataTools Pro API endpoints (`app.datatoolspro.com/.../api/`), the application returns an HTML login page instead of a structured JSON error response. This is problematic for API consumers and makes it difficult to detect and respond to abuse patterns.

## Requirements

### JSON Error Responses
All API endpoints must return JSON-formatted error responses with appropriate HTTP status codes, regardless of the error type (authentication failure, validation error, malformed request). The Laravel Sanctum default behavior of redirecting unauthenticated requests to the login page must be overridden.

### Rate Limiting and Abuse Detection
After 10 incorrect API key attempts, the offending IP should be locked out at the firewall level and an alert triggered. This signals to Cloudflare WAF that invalid or malicious requests are being made. The behavior applies to:
- Direct requests to `api.datatoolspro.com`
- Requests via integration channels (Zapier, Coda)

### Request Validation
Malformed requests should be caught and rejected at the PHP layer before hitting the database, returning a JSON error with a descriptive error code.
acceptance_criteria: All API endpoints return JSON error responses instead of HTML pages
Unauthenticated requests return a JSON 401 error instead of redirecting to the login page
Invalid or expired API tokens return a JSON error with a descriptive message
Malformed request payloads are rejected with JSON validation errors before database queries
Error responses include an HTTP status code and descriptive error message
Rate limiting triggers after 10 failed authentication attempts from the same IP
Locked-out IPs receive a JSON 429 or 403 response
Alerts are generated when an IP is locked out for investigation
middleware_changes: Sanctum's default unauthenticated redirect overridden to return JSON 401 responses. Request validation layer added to reject malformed payloads before they reach the database. Rate limiting middleware tracks failed auth attempts per IP.
api_changes: All API error responses now return structured JSON with HTTP status codes and descriptive messages. Applies to authentication errors, validation errors, and unexpected server errors across all versioned API endpoints.
implementation_notes: The root cause was Sanctum's default behavior: unauthenticated requests were redirected to the login page (HTML). This was overridden to return a JSON 401 response. The rate limiting and WAF integration with Cloudflare was planned to detect patterns of malicious requests across both direct API access and integration channels (Zapier, Coda). The goal is to prevent brute-force API key attacks and protect the database from unnecessary load.
comment_summary: Waqar identified that Sanctum's default redirect was causing the HTML response issue and fixed it to return JSON errors. A screenshot of the corrected JSON error response for invalid tokens was shared for review.
